channel.title: Cubeler Industrial News Monitor
channel.description: Monthly industrial news compilation across 10 sectors for February 2026.

item.title: Federal Food Affordability Announcement and Industry Reaction
item.description: The federal government announced a 25% increase to the GST credit and a $150 million Food Security Fund; Restaurants Canada responds with calls for a total GST exemption on restaurant meals.

item.title: The Rise of Agentic AI in Restaurant Operations
item.description: A deep dive into how AI agents are moving beyond simple chatbots to autonomously managing inventory and scheduling for Canadian operators.

item.title: Are GLP-1s Driving Canadian Menu Changes?
item.description: With 2 million Canadians now using GLP-1 weight-loss medications, researchers examine the shift toward smaller "grazing" portions in restaurants.

item.title: Authenticity and Intentionality Drive 2026 Menu Trends
item.description: Consumer demand for "clean labels" and authentic cultural storytelling is redefining top-performing menus this year.

item.title: Puratos Opens First Canadian Bakery School
item.description: A new culinary institution has launched in Canada to address the specialized labor shortage in the professional baking sector.

item.title: Yes Shef Culinary Event Makes Ontario Debut
item.description: The "Yes Shef" event spotlights women in the hospitality and tourism industry, celebrating leadership and mentorship.

item.title: CaféTO Curb Lane Program Deadlines
item.description: Toronto restaurant operators must finalize applications for the 2026 curb lane patio season by mid-February.

item.title: Protein Packs a Punch on 2026 Restaurant Menus
item.description: High-protein ingredients like mung beans and pulses are trending as functional nutrition becomes a diner priority.

item.title: Franchising Trends: Opportunities for Growth in 2026
item.description: Hotelier Magazine analyzes how franchising is becoming a preferred expansion route for major hotel brands in a high-interest environment.

item.title: Dynamic Pricing Models Dissected
item.description: An analysis of new algorithmic pricing tools helping independent hotels compete with global chains on room rate elasticity.

item.title: Ag Canada Expands AgriStability to Include Pasture-Related Feed Costs
item.description: A major policy shift now allows livestock producers to claim feed costs on rented pasture under the safety net program.

item.title: Wheat Pete’s Word: Compaction Consequences and Manure on Snow
item.description: Agronomy expert Peter Johnson warns of the yield risks associated with early-season soil compaction and improper manure application.

item.title: FMC Launches Avireo: New Pre-Seed Herbicide for Cereals
item.description: A new chemical tool has been approved for the 2026 season to combat glufosinate-resistant weeds in cereal crops.

item.title: 82% of Farmers Say They’ll Have Access to Capital in 2026
item.description: Despite high rates, a majority of Canadian farmers remain optimistic about securing the necessary growth capital for spring seeding.

item.title: Minister MacDonald and Secretary Rollins Meet Face-to-Face
item.description: Canada’s Ag Minister quietly met with the U.S. Secretary of Agriculture to discuss trade stability and tariff concerns.

item.title: Canada Secures Expanded Market Access for Meat to Indonesia
item.description: Indonesia has opened its doors to Canadian pork and 'over-thirty-month' bone-in beef products.

item.title: CFA: Canadians Worked First 39 Days of 2026 to Pay for Groceries
item.description: 'Food Freedom Day' falls on Feb. 8, highlighting the growing burden of food costs on household disposable income.

item.title: Jason Leblond Named Beef Farmers of Ontario President
item.description: The BFO has appointed new leadership focused on advancing the Beef Breeder Loan Program and provincial traceability.

item.title: Glufosinate Resistance Threatens Fight for Weed Freedom
item.description: Researchers warn that increasing herbicide resistance is putting pressure on traditional canola and soybean rotations.

item.title: Soybean School: Soybean Cyst Nematode Lessons from Brazil
item.description: A cross-border research update provides new strategies for Canadian growers to manage nematode infestations.

item.title: SiteAI Launches Custom AI Tools for Industrial Construction
item.description: A new software platform aims to automate site safety inspections and equipment tracking for large-scale projects.

item.title: Military Aims to Build 7,500 Housing Units Across Canada
item.description: The Canadian Armed Forces are accelerating a massive housing initiative to provide dedicated units for military families.

item.title: 4 Takeaways from Canada’s New Defence Industrial Strategy
item.description: Construction experts analyze how the new federal strategy will prioritize domestic infrastructure and 'Build-Partner-Buy' frameworks.

item.title: Institutional Capital Returns to Canadian Commercial Real Estate
item.description: Major pension funds are re-entering the market as commercial valuations stabilize in early 2026.

item.title: Ottawa New Home Sales Remain Under Pressure
item.description: High inventory and buyer hesitation are keeping new residential sales in the nation's capital below seasonal norms.

item.title: 30-Storey Mixed-Use Tower Proposed for St. Laurent Boulevard
item.description: Developers have filed plans for a major intensification project in Ottawa's east end.

item.title: Guelph and GRCA Partner to Secure 126-Acre Niska Road Site
item.description: A strategic land acquisition has been finalized to protect future green space and public infrastructure.

item.title: Major Contracts Awarded for Matawinie Mine Work
item.description: Infrastructure firms have secured key civil engineering and construction mandates for the northern graphite mine.

item.title: Site Service Awards Ceremony in Vancouver
item.description: Industry leaders gather to recognize excellence in construction service and operational safety.

item.title: Ontario Dominated This Year’s Top 100 Projects List
item.description: With $343B in project value, Ontario remains the primary hub for Canadian infrastructure development.

item.title: Wellness Wins: Why 94% of Members Can’t Live Without Their Gym
item.description: A comprehensive 2026 study highlights the 'essentialization' of fitness as a core part of mental and physical wellness.

item.title: Impact Magazine Releases Canada’s Top Fitness Trainers Class of 2026
item.description: Recognition of the 36 top professionals nationwide who are leading innovation in functional health.

item.title: canfitpro Returns to Montreal with fitMONTRÉAL One-Day Event
item.description: The largest Canadian fitness professional association hosts a boutique-focused educational event in Quebec.

item.title: Rethinking Exercise for Long-Term Health
item.description: Wellness experts urge a move away from 'all-or-nothing' workouts toward sustainable, daily physical integration.

item.title: New Partnership Explores the Power of Pea Protein for Healthy Aging
item.description: A research collaboration explores plant-based nutrition's role in maintaining muscle mass in the elderly.

item.title: Bridging the Gap to Better Nutrition: The Food Access Bus Pilot
item.description: A new transit-based initiative brings fresh produce directly to wellness deserts in urban neighborhoods.

item.title: Recovering After Heart Surgery: Jemalyn’s Story
item.description: A patient case study highlighting the success of integrated cardiovascular rehabilitation programs.

item.title: ECW Ratio: The Hidden Metric for Assessing Muscle Quality
item.description: A technical guide for health professionals on using Bioelectrical Impedance to measure intracellular fluid and muscle health.

item.title: PACMan Pilot Study: Shaping the Future of Healthy Aging
item.description: Call for participants in a new study focusing on digital wellness interventions for older adults.

item.title: Why Heart Health Month Needs Solutions, Not Slogans
item.description: Industry leaders call for actionable policy changes rather than simple awareness campaigns to combat heart disease.

item.title: Premium Brands to Acquire Stampede Culinary Partners
item.description: A $662.5 million deal expands Canada's protein and culinary solution platform into the U.S. market.

item.title: Swiss Bakery Cornu to Open New Facility in Edmundston
item.description: A $32.5 million artisan baked goods facility is set to create local jobs in New Brunswick.

item.title: NRCan Unveils Retrofit Hub for Institutional Owners
item.description: A new centralized platform launched to help manufacturers and building owners finance and implement energy retrofits.

item.title: ams Osram and Ledvance Extend Licensing Partnership Beyond 2026
item.description: The global lighting giants commit to a long-term partnership to advance Osram-branded products globally.

item.title: Cancoil Invests Over $16 Million to Enhance Production Capacity
item.description: The refrigeration manufacturer is installing new state-of-the-art automated equipment in its Ontario facilities.

item.title: Inter Function to Acquire New Automated Equipment for Door Production
item.description: A repayable federal contribution supports the purchase of new automated inspection and production tools.

item.title: Norbec’s New Strathroy Facility Designed as Highly Automated Operation
item.description: Norbec officially opened its $45-million manufacturing plant in Ontario, focusing on insulated metal panels.

item.title: Manufacturing Sector Stabilizes in January (S&P Global PMI)
item.description: Data indicates the sector has returned to expansion territory for the first time in 12 months.

item.title: Schneider Electric EcoFit Center Opens
item.description: Milton facility to focus on 'circularity' and electrical modernization R&D.

item.title: Mars-Kellanova Merger Clears All Regulatory Hurdles
item.description: The multi-billion dollar acquisition is finalized, creating a new global leader in the snack and snacking category.

item.title: Hazelview Begins Strategic Review for Four Quadrant Fund
item.description: A review is underway to explore portfolio sales or recapitalization for the massive real estate fund to unlock unitholder value.

item.title: The Great Decoupling in Industrial Real Estate Leasing
item.description: Analysis shows a widening gap in lease rates between Class-A and Class-C properties in the Greater Toronto Area.

item.title: Station House: New Rental Towers Launch in Toronto
item.description: Hazelview and Fitzrovia have launched leasing for two major rental towers within the Bloor and Dufferin project.

item.title: Calgary Market Update February 2026: Balanced Conditions Return
item.description: While sales are down 15% YoY, inventory has hit a six-year high, shifting power back toward buyers.

item.title: CIBC Square Both Towers Fully Leased
item.description: Despite wider office market uncertainty, CIBC Square has hit 100% occupancy for its downtown towers.

item.title: Real Capital 2026 Conference Highlights
item.description: Institutional investors gather in Toronto to discuss the impact of stabilizing interest rates on national portfolios.

item.title: Housing Market Outlook 2026: Resilient Starts Amid Uncertainty
item.description: CMHC report suggests housing starts will remain higher than historic averages despite economic cooling.

item.title: RE/MAX: Confident First-Time Buyers Returning to Market
item.description: Lowered rates and stable prices are driving a surge in first-time buyer inquiries across major Canadian metros.

item.title: Atlantic Canada’s Strategic Stability Favors Long-Term Investors
item.description: Nova Scotia and New Brunswick markets show less volatility than central Canada, attracting institutional capital.

item.title: Vancouver Real Estate Forum: MIPIM Preview
item.description: Regional leaders prepare for the global urban festival with a focus on sustainable densification.

item.title: Daily Synopsis: Retail Expansion and Innovation Signal Resilience
item.description: Amazon launches AI-powered Creative Agent in Canada to help retailers automate high-quality ad creation.

item.title: Loblaw Retail Revenue Surpasses $16 Billion in 2025
item.description: The grocery giant reports record year-end results while committing to 77 new store openings.

item.title: Canada’s Grocery Store Density is Declining
item.description: Despite major expansions, the number of supermarkets per capita is falling as discount stores replace traditional formats.

item.title: Quebec Retail Hours Pilot Expands Weekend Shopping
item.description: New provincial pilot allows stores to stay open later on weekends to compete with e-commerce giants.

item.title: Valentine’s Day Spending in Canada Jumps 48%
item.description: Moneris data shows a massive week-over-week spending spike led by florists and restaurants.

item.title: Walmart Canada Supercentre Expansion
item.description: Two new 140,000-sq.-ft. locations in Ontario and Alberta are set to open as part of a $1B national strategy.

item.title: Roots Appoints Rosie Pouzar as Chief Commercial Officer
item.description: The iconic brand taps new leadership to advance global commercial execution and growth strategy.

item.title: Jimmy John’s Opens First Saskatoon Location
item.description: The sandwich giant continues its aggressive Canadian expansion with its first site in the Saskatchewan market.

item.title: AI Adoption Accelerates Across Canadian Retail
item.description: Major retailers shift from AI pilots to full-scale 'agentic commerce' for inventory and pricing.

item.title: Amazon Expands Same-Day Delivery Options
item.description: New urban hubs allow for same-day delivery on over 450 million items across major Canadian cities.

item.title: NordSpace Launches Venture Arm with Strategic Investment in Wyvern
item.description: The space-tech firm invests in Edmonton’s satellite technology to advance high-resolution Rosette imaging.

item.title: OpenAI Connection to Tumbler Ridge Tragedy Puts Privacy in Spotlight
item.description: Regulators investigate why OpenAI failed to alert police to shooter-related ChatGPT logs prior to a fatal event.

item.title: Private Equity Firm Novacap Closes Seventh and Largest Tech Fund
item.description: A massive $3.8 billion USD fund has been closed to target majority stakes in profitable Canadian tech firms.

item.title: New Alliance Positioning Edmonton as a Canadian Defence Hub
item.description: Organizations unite to pursue federal contracts under the new Defence Industrial Strategy.

item.title: Can Canada Find Digital Sovereignty in the Fediverse?
item.description: Advocates at FediMTL argue for a decentralized 'social web' to combat the influence of global Big Tech.

item.title: As Vacancies Rise, Padder Aims to Insure Rent Payments
item.description: A new 'guarantor-as-a-service' startup launches to help non-traditional tenants secure Ontario housing.

item.title: L-Spark Completes First Cohort as a Corporate Accelerator
item.description: Eight companies have successfully integrated new telecom solutions into Mitel’s global infrastructure.

item.title: Alberta-Made Robots are Helping Keep Vancouver Shipyards Safe
item.description: Deep-tech robotics from Edmonton are being deployed for hazardous site inspections on the West Coast.

item.title: General Magic Secures $7.2 Million USD for Insurance AI Agents
item.description: The Toronto startup uses AI agents to automate customer communications via text and voice.

item.title: Neo Financial Partners with United Airlines to Launch Credit Card
item.description: The fintech disruptor beats out major traditional banks to secure a massive airline partnership.

item.title: Ministers Agree to Deal with Driver Inc. Across Canada
item.description: Provincial and federal leaders commit to coordinated enforcement against employee misclassification in trucking.

item.title: Commitment to Solve 14 Key Interprovincial Trade Barriers
item.description: Ministers sign an MoU to streamline regulatory hurdles for cross-border hauling between provinces.

item.title: DOT Forces 550 Truck Driver Training Schools to Close
item.description: A major safety sweep identifies hundreds of unqualified schools failing to meet federal training standards.

item.title: CVTA Chairman: Reputable Schools Welcome Decertification of Bad Players
item.description: Industry leaders support the crackdown on 'sham schools' to improve national road safety and driver value.

item.title: CTA: Survey Confirms Wage Theft and Dangerous Conditions Warnings
item.description: A new national survey of drivers highlights ongoing labor abuse within the Driver Inc. model.

item.title: Ottawa Signals Tougher Transparency for Non-Compliant Firms
item.description: New legislation will require public disclosure of trucking firms failing to meet safety and tax audits.

item.title: Strategic Response Urged to U.S. EPA Emission Regulation Rescission
item.description: The CTA warns that Canada must align its GHG strategy as the U.S. eliminates key trucking emission rules.

item.title: Gordie Howe Bridge Hailed as Economic Backbone for Allies
item.description: Officials highlight the bridge as a vital artery for the $100B North American agri-food corridor.

item.title: Trucking Associations Praise DOT Decisive Action on Safety
item.description: The ATA and OOIDA release statements supporting the removal of 550 training providers from the registry.

item.title: Established Schools Pass First Enforcement Audits Since 2022
item.description: Reputable training centers celebrate the first actual enforcement of federal standards passed four years ago.

item.title: Ransomware Surges as Auto Cyberattacks Double
item.description: Auto cyberattacks doubled in 2025, with ransomware now accounting for 44% of incidents.

item.title: B.C. Dealers Say Budget 2026 Misses Transportation Reality
item.description: The NCDA argues the new budget fails to address EV charging infrastructure and technician shortages.

item.title: 92% of Automotive Cyberattacks Conducted Remotely
item.description: New data reveals that physical proximity is no longer required for massive-scale breaches of vehicle systems.

item.title: Technician Shortage Placing Upward Pressure on Ownership Costs
item.description: A lack of specialized EV technicians is driving up maintenance and repair labor rates across Canada.

item.title: NCDA Renews Call to Revisit $55,000 Luxury Tax Threshold
item.description: With the average new-vehicle price exceeding $66k, dealers argue the tax is unfairly hitting modern essentials.

item.title: 67% of Auto Incidents Involved Telematics and Cloud Systems
item.description: The Upstream report highlights that cloud vulnerabilities are the primary entry point for wholesale data breaches.

item.title: Retail Sales in B.C. represent $17 Billion and 30,000 Jobs
item.description: The NCDA emphasizes the wholesale sector's vital role in the provincial economy during budget talks.

item.title: Ransomware Expanding Beyond Enterprise to Vehicles Themselves
item.description: Attackers have begun locking owners out of vehicles via companion apps, demanding payment for access.

item.title: B.C. ZEV Framework Alignment with Federal Policy Welcomed
item.description: Dealers applaud the clarity provided by aligning provincial zero-emission targets with national standards.

item.title: Average Used Car Price in Canada Rises Amidst Inventory Gaps
item.description: Persistent wholesale inventory shortages continue to drive up resale values for late-model vehicles.
